steps.title: Isolate the absolute value
steps.content: Start with $$-5|6x+7|+5=-30.$$ Subtract $5$ from both sides: $$-5|6x+7|=-35.$$ Divide by $-5$: $$|6x+7|=7.$$

steps.title: Solve the two cases
steps.content: So $6x+7=7$ or $6x+7=-7$. The first gives $6x=0$, so $x=0$. The second gives $6x=-14$, so $x=-\frac{7}{3}$. Both values make $|6x+7|=7$, so the solution set matches the correct answer, C.
